food_subgroup: Steamed rice flour kueh with coconut or peanut filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Kueh Tutu. Nearest parent is a steamed rice flour cake or rice confectionery. A new FoodOn concept is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice-based confectionery — steamed rice flour cake with coconut or nut filling (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Kueh Tutu consists of 3 pieces (approximately 30g each, 90g total) of small, white, dome-shaped steamed rice flour cakes with a sweet shredded coconut or ground peanut filling, served wrapped in a piece of banana leaf or pandan leaf.

nutrient_source.title: Kueh Tutu — HPB Singapore Foodo Database; no exact code found; values adapted from HPB rice flour kueh and coconut/peanut filling composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Kueh Tutu entry found in HPB Foodo. Values adapted from HPB steamed rice flour cake and coconut filling data. Groundnut version has slightly higher protein and fat.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — fine rice flour, steamed; GI elevated by gelatinised rice starch similar to putu piring.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 70mg per 3-piece serving (90g) — about 3% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit; one of the lowest-sodium traditional kueh.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension may consume Kueh Tutu occasionally; the sodium content is negligible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Fine rice flour base has a high GI (~72); per-serving glycaemic load of approximately 26 from just 3 pieces is significant; sugar from filling adds further glycaemic impact.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit Kueh Tutu to 1–2 pieces as an occasional treat; the high GI rice flour base and sweet filling together produce a significant postprandial glucose rise disproportionate to the small portion size.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content may promote inflammation at frequent consumption; coconut provides medium-chain saturated fatty acids.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Kueh Tutu to occasional treats due to the high sugar and refined rice flour content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kueh Tutu is one of Singapore's most traditional and nostalgic street kueh — small, white dome-shaped steamed cakes made from finely ground rice flour pressed into metal mould plates with a sweet filling of either freshly shredded coconut with sugar, or ground roasted peanuts with sugar. The moulds are placed over a steamer and the kueh is ready in minutes, served fresh in a small banana or pandan leaf wrapper. Kueh Tutu stalls, once common throughout Singapore, are now rare heritage fixtures, making them a sought-after nostalgic treat at cultural events and selected hawker centres.
common_user_queries: how many calories in kueh tutu
can a diabetic eat kueh tutu
kueh tutu coconut vs peanut which is healthier
where to buy kueh tutu Singapore

confidence_reason: No exact Kueh Tutu entry found in HPB Foodo. Values adapted from HPB steamed rice flour cake and filling component data. Confidence medium.
notes: Coconut version is listed here. Groundnut version has slightly higher protein (5.5g/100g), higher fat (8.5g/100g), and lower saturated fat ratio. Both versions are fully vegan, gluten-free, and allergen-free (coconut version). This is the final record in Batch 1 of the Project Veritas initiative.
